reasoning:
The objective in the Keep_Track Project 2025 Tracker is to "Break up the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)." As of mid-May 2025 (less than two months before the July 1 resolution date), the tracker still has this objective entirely blank, despite ongoing budget cuts, layoffs, and the termination of some advisory boards. The tracker is conservative about marking things complete—for example, major structural changes to the CFPB are marked "in progress." 

Crucially, in January 2025, Commerce Secretary nominee Howard Lutnick officially disavowed the Project 2025 plan to break up NOAA. Any actual attempt to break up the agency requires congressional approval (as noted in an April 2025 NYT report about White House budget plans to dismantle NOAA research), a process taking many months or years, especially with deep pushback from Democrats and some Republicans. Even if the administration pursues this heavily over the next six weeks, it would at most result in an "in progress" mark, not "complete," which is required for a YES resolution. The timeline is simply too tight for an objective of this magnitude to be achieved and marked complete by the Keep_Track operators.
